title: Explicit SC/VSC Detector With Regime-Gated Blend
summary: FIA SC/VSC procedures create a different decision regime, and official F1 analysis explicitly notes that cheap Safety Car stops change optimal pit timing.
rationale: FIA SC/VSC procedures create a different decision regime, and official F1 analysis explicitly notes that cheap Safety Car stops change optimal pit timing. A concrete neutralization detector is a sharper, testable version of the earlier hidden-regime idea.
implementation_hint: Detect likely SC/VSC laps from field-wide lap-time inflation, suppressed position-change variance, and pit-stop clustering. Train separate green-flag and neutralized specialists, then gate or OOF rank-blend them using the detector probability; if you calibrate, prefer rank-preserving sigmoid or pure rank blending for AUC.
expected_effect: Most useful on rare but high-leverage pit windows, with potential LB gain from handling regime shifts that current single-model heuristics smear together.
risk: A noisy detector can send rows to the wrong expert, and isotonic post-calibration can reduce ROC AUC by introducing ties.
